expect: Mid-cycle EAD: >=3 predictions trending toward target. GLD BULL needs confidence downgrade given -3.1% vs +5% target.

actual.summary: 2/7 trackable trending toward target (DXY, OIL), 4 flat, 1 against (GLD). 8/18 missing base_price. Safe-haven thesis failing across gold/bonds/utilities despite active Hormuz closure.

PRED-0004.reason: Gold -3.1% vs +5% target. Safe-haven thesis failing.

key_findings: Highest-confidence prediction (GLD 70%) is worst performer — overconfidence signal
Safe-haven thesis failing: gold down, bonds flat, utilities -4% despite active war
Oil thesis validated ($93.55, Hormuz closed) but XLE flat — sector decoupling
8/18 predictions missing base_price — market_predict.py data quality gap
DXY weakening is clearest trend-toward-target (-0.5%, YoY -4.4%)
actual.macro_context: Mar 20 2026: broad selloff (SPY -1.7%, QQQ -1.85%, EEM -3.4%, IWM -2.2%, XLU -4.1%). VIX +11.3% to 26.78. Oil rallied to $93.55 (Hormuz closed). DXY 99.50. Market pricing WAR + dollar stress but NOT safe-haven rotation.

diff: Expected >=3 trending: got 2 (DXY, OIL). GLD downgrade 0.70->0.50 confirmed. Surprise: safe-haven thesis failing across ALL safe-haven assets simultaneously despite Hormuz closure. Anomalous — suggests dollar-liquidity squeeze or conflict already fully priced. This is the most informative finding: our macro model assumed war=gold+bonds+utilities UP, but the actual market response is sector-indiscriminate selling.
